Welcome everyone to the longer version of the Diagnostic Sale. It is a two-page sale. Try to keep this as short and as easy as humanly possible. I think that you should print this out on double-sided paper and put them on clipboards so that the person who is actually doing the sale can literally read the questions. So the intention of this sale is to make this as scalable as possible and as easy as possible to delegate. I know that Dylan already handed this over to a salesperson and they closed like a $2400 sale in their first try who I've never sold before. So if you don't overthink this, it is a very, very, very simple process. And so you're literally just going to ask the questions. The reason I set it up this way was with the little black dots is because I think with your eyes it's easier to kind of check off the questions that you've already asked. And then you can write answers in the little gap that's underneath. And that way it looks like it's much more of an intake kind of questionnaire back and forth rather than you interrogating them or selling them. So I have the pre-meeting checklist just to make sure that any of your people who are working with you have already done the, they filled out the pre-framed questionnaire, they noted their bad habit cost section, making sure that we've covered. And so great. So what brought you in today? And usually these questions you can ask in a series quickly because people take multiple questions to understand what we're trying to say. So we're like, cool, we're watching your way. What seems to be the problem? What's your goal? What are you trying to accomplish? So I'll usually ask all four of those questions at once and then they'll just tell me, I'm trying to lose weight. I feel kind of shitty and you're like, okay, got it.
